name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Baton Rouge, La
description_short: NACA is a national non-profit housing counselor providing character-based mortgage lending and HUD housing counseling services to underserved communities, with no down payment, closing costs, or credit score requirements.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has evolved into a major national non-profit focused on closing the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership. The organization has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 homeowners, and originated 75,000 mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments. NACA provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally, making it one of the largest housing counselors in the United States.

NACA's primary offering is their character-based mortgage program that eliminates traditional barriers to homeownership. They provide mortgages with no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no consideration of credit score—only a full review of the applicant's circumstances. Current rates (as of April 2026) are 5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year mortgages, positioned as below-market fixed rates. The organization operates "Achieve the Dream" events in multiple cities where potential members can meet with NACA staff, complete counseling, and become NACA Qualified for mortgage access.

What distinguishes NACA is their explicit mission-driven approach to addressing predatory lending and systemic inequality. They combine mortgage lending with advocacy work, including campaigns against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors. NACA operates member and volunteer portals, engages in policy advocacy through their founder Bruce Marks' public platform, and accepts financial donations to support their work. Their character-based lending model directly contrasts with traditional credit-focused lending and explicitly serves low-to-moderate income people and communities of color historically excluded from homeownership.

However, NACA's model requires significant member commitment and involvement. Applicants must attend multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events (typically Friday-Sunday), participate in counseling, and engage as active members or "NACtivist" volunteers. This is not a quick online application process. Additionally, while their rates are advertised as below-market, they're not the lowest available in all scenarios, and membership carries expectations of community participation and advocacy engagement aligned with NACA's mission.
pros: No down payment required on mortgages—removes primary barrier to homeownership
No closing costs or mortgage insurance—saves thousands in upfront expenses
Character-based lending that ignores credit scores—serves those with poor credit history
Below-market fixed rates currently at 5.625% (30-year) with transparent pricing
Provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally—established credibility in HUD system
Serves 500,000+ homeowners with $20B in mortgage commitments—proven track record
Free HUD housing counseling included—comprehensive financial guidance beyond lending
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day in-person "Achieve the Dream" events (Friday-Sunday) for qualification—not suitable for those unable to travel or take time off work
Membership involves expectations of activism and community participation—not purely transactional
Limited transparency on website about specific income limits, debt-to-income ratios, or other underwriting criteria
Geographic event limitation—only available in select cities on scheduled dates, potentially requiring long-distance travel
Below-market rates may still not be the absolute lowest available compared to all mortgage lenders nationally
best_for: Low-to-moderate income individuals and families with poor or no credit history seeking first-time homeownership
Communities of color historically excluded from traditional mortgage lending due to discriminatory practices
Individuals with limited savings who cannot afford down payments or closing costs
Borrowers seeking counseling and guidance alongside affordable mortgage financing
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with poor credit or limited savings who can commit to attending in-person counseling events and engaging in the organization's mission-driven community. The primary caveat is that NACA requires significant time and member participation commitment—this is not a quick online lending process, and applicants must travel to scheduled 'Achieve the Dream' events in specific cities.
services: Character-based mortgage lending with no down payment required
Fixed-rate mortgages (15-year, 20-year, 30-year) with no closing costs or fees
HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of national HUD counseling)
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events for member qualification and counseling
Member portal for document upload, file access, and status tracking
NACtivist volunteer platform for community organizing and advocacy
Advocacy campaigns against predatory lending and corporate landlords
Mortgage rate locking and commitment services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 10101 Siegen Ln Bldg 2 Ste B, Baton Rouge, LA 70810
